description: A two-panel split-screen comparison infographic contrasting inflation under Biden versus Trump. The top half shows a profile photograph of Joe Biden in a dark suit and blue striped tie against blurred U.S. flags; overlaid on the right is a large red box reading '9.1%' with white serif text below: 'Inflation under Biden' and smaller text 'Highest during presidency - June 2022', plus an attribution '@PressSec on X'. A thin divider band with the engraved White House logo separates the panels. The bottom half shows a photograph of Trump in a dark overcoat and red scarf speaking at a presidential podium (seal visible) against red-and-white striped bunting with flowers in the foreground; overlaid at left is a large green box reading '2.7%' with white serif text 'Inflation under Trump' and smaller 'Average during presidency', plus 'White House' attribution. The design uses the classic red=bad / green=good color coding to draw a favorable contrast. Messaging technique: selective statistical comparison (peak Biden figure vs. Trump average) to credit the administration with lowering inflation; clean editorial infographic style. Target audience: general public and economic-policy voters.
notable_text: 9.1% Inflation under Biden (Highest during presidency - June 2022); 2.7% Inflation under Trump (Average during presidency)
